text:
There once was a frog named Fergus who lived on the banks of a small, sparkling creek. He had a job delivering pesky mosquitos to fish. One summer afternoon, the mosquitos came out early, buzzing around Fergus.

"Give them a buzz!", he said jovially to his friend the dragonfly.

As he flew from insect to insect, Fergus felt a gentle nibble on his leg. Backing away, he swatted the mosquito away. It bounced toward another. He chased after it. Before he could reach it, he passed something odd — a tiny frog who looked just like him.

"Oh! Hello there!" Fergus gasped. The tiny frog giggled. "Hey there! You must be new around here. Name's Pip."

"Why am I chasing mosquitoes?" Fergus inquired.

"Pip?" Pip quivered. "Because they're delicious but messy, and they love landing on your feet."
